1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **Problem:** Writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` to a file on every build. Since the timestamp always changes, the file is always "modified," which invalidates downstream targets (especially `CoreCompile`).
- **Also missing:** `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild can never skip it.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **Problem:** Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes — MSBuild will always execute it.
- **Secondary issue:** Even if `Inputs`/`Outputs` were added, `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` updates the file timestamp every build, triggering recompilation even when the git hash hasn't changed.

### Root Causes Summary

| Issue | Effect |
|-------|--------|
| No `Inputs`/`Outputs` on either target | MSBuild never skips them |
| Volatile `DateTime::Now` in output | File always differs → forces recompile |
| Unconditional file writes | Touch file timestamps even if content unchanged |

### Recommended Fixes

1. **Remove or condition the timestamp target** — volatile values are fundamentally incompatible with incremental builds.
2. **Add `Inputs`/`Outputs`** to `EmbedGitHash` and use a **write-if-changed** pattern (write to a `.tmp` file, then `Copy` only if content differs) so `CoreCompile` isn't invalidated when the hash hasn't changed.
